summary: Extend clean_bodies.py: {p. roman} split page markers, standalone p. NN paragraphs, [N] footnote refs
description: Phase-1 audit (docs/summary/readability-audit.md, todo:82e3a09d) found the biggest regex-fixable surface: egyptian-book-of-the-dead-index (357 chunks, mean 5.8) has {p.\n\nxcli} page markers broken across paragraph boundaries, [1]/[2] footnote refs, and stray unbalanced brackets; kojiki/yasna-47/dhammapada-16 single chunks have p. NN remnants; mandaean prose chunks share the p. NN pattern. Add pattern classes (split-across-newline-aware) to clean_bodies.py, respect the shrink guard, re-run scripts/audit_readability.py to confirm score drops, then re-export. Do NOT touch gilgamesh/hermeticism brackets — verified legitimate translator reconstruction markers.
